findings.problem: Major turns in the hearing keep arriving through the same copular bridge move: 'That was...', 'There it was.', or 'It was...'. On a chapter built to feel contested and freshly dangerous, those repeated launch pads make the rhetoric audible as a reusable template instead of letting each turn feel discovered under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the bridge beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:13, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:103, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:177, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:217, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:263, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:313, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:335, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:421 so the pivot comes first from local physical reality already present in the scene: the pen, the fan, the parcel map, a body movement, the heat, the folded order, the chair scrape. Keep the interpretive turn, but re-syntax most of these paragraphs so they do not all announce themselves with another 'That/There/It was' bridge.
findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:103, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:177, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:217, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:263, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:313, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:335, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:421, no more than one paragraph may open with a copular bridge ('That/There/It was'). The rest must pivot from a concrete observable trigger in the room or hall and still preserve the same causal meaning.

findings.problem: The prose leans too often on the same comparison-and-correction scaffolds: frequent 'as if' clauses, repeated 'not X but Y' reversals, and clipped 'Not X. Y.' emphases. Individually they are effective; in aggregate they become an audible tic and flatten the chapter into self-imitation at exactly the moments that should feel most alive.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the repetitive scaffolds around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:27, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:79, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:141, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:159, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:177, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:267, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:319, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:383, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:447. Keep only the strongest figurative or contrastive turns; replace several others with direct sensory statement, action, or unfigured judgment so each paragraph lands with a different sentence shape rather than another version of the same rhetorical hinge.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised clusters anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:27, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:79, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:177, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:267, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:319, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:383, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:447, each local beat should use at most one 'as if' or contrastive self-correction ('not X but Y' or 'Not X. Y.'). At least three of those passages should now land through plain declarative observation with no comparison scaffold at all.

summary: Booker and dialogue pass: the chapter cleanly cashes in the hearing spine, preserves past-tense continuity, and keeps Della, Vernon, Noreen, Lucille, and Rafe distinct in register and contraction use. Craft and prose fail because sentence-level autopilot is too audible: repeated 'That/There/It was' bridges and repeated comparison/contrast templates ('as if', 'Not X. Y.', 'not X but Y') recur often enough to flatten an otherwise strong chapter into a self-echoing rhetorical pattern.
